title: La Voix Humaine / Point d’orgue
subtitle: Patricia Petibon, Jean-Sébastien Bou, Cyrille Dubois, Olivier Py, Pierre-André Weitz, Bertrand Killy, Ariane Matiakh Ariane Matiakh (dir.)
description: _Orchestre National de France

title: Orchestre Philharmonique de Strasbourg / Aziz Shokhakimov
subtitle: Jean-Guihen Queyras - Alexandre Tharaud - Strasnoy, Chostakovitch
description: A new work by Oscar Strasnoy, with Jean-Guihen Queyras and Alexandre Tharaud as soloists, sets the stage for Shostakovich’s monumental Seventh Symphony—his longest and one of his most intense compositions, ending in a fiercely triumphant climax.

title: Rendez-vous avec Mahler
subtitle: Orchestre national d’Île-de-France - Case Scaglione - Éric-Emmanuel Schmitt
description: The Orchestre National d’Île-de-France, with its musical director Case Scaglione conducting, is joined by Éric-Emmanuel Schmitt for an evening dedicated to Mahler’s profound and majestic Ninth Symphony.

title: La Voix Humaine / Point d’orgue
subtitle: Patricia Petibon, Jean-Sébastien Bou, Cyrille Dubois, Olivier Py, Pierre-André Weitz, Bertrand Killy, Ariane Matiakh Ariane Matiakh (dir.)
description: _Orchestre National de France

title: Orchestre de Paris / Klaus Mäkelä
subtitle: Yunchan Lim - Rachmaninoff
description: It certainly takes the quick-silver fingers of Yunchan Lim to embark on the adventure of Rachmaninov's Concerto No.2, accompanied this evening by the splendid Symphony No.2 and a fascinating transcription.

title: Nuit sans aube / Matthias Pintscher
subtitle: James Darrah, Adam Rigg, Molly Irelan, Yi Zhao, Hana Kim, Evan Hughes, Marie-Adeline Henry, Jennifer Johnston, Catherine Trottmann, Julie Robard-Gendre, Matthias Pintscher Matthias Pintscher (dir.)

subtitle: Yefim Bronfman - Chin, Schumann, Stravinski
description: A programme bursting with colour, featuring the kaleidoscopic styles of Unsuk Chin’s Frontispiece, the passion and tenderness of a Romantic concerto gem, and the sharp wit and inventiveness of a Stravinsky masterpiece.

title: American dreams
subtitle: Orchestre Pasdeloup - Christian Vásquez - Mihkel Poll - Copland, Rachmaninoff, Price
description: Alongside Rachmaninov’s monumental Third Piano Concerto, Christian Vásquez conducts works by American composers Aaron Copland and Florence Price, the first African-American woman composer to have her work performed by major orchestras.

subtitle: Mozart, Bruckner
description: A masterful interpreter of the German repertoire, Marek Janowski leads the Orchestre de l’Opéra de Paris in a programme pairing one of Mozart’s finest symphonies with one of Bruckner’s most resplendent orchestral masterpieces.

description: With Emmanuelle Bertrand as soloist, Elgar’s iconic Cello Concerto unfolds in all its depth, before the crystalline sonorities and lyrical sweep of Prokofiev’s Romeo and Juliet offer a spellbinding counterpoint.

title: Filarmonica della Scala - Milan / Riccardo Chailly
subtitle: Alexandre Kantorow - Prokofiev, Tchaïkovski
description: A summit meeting between the Italian maestro and the French pianist in Prokofiev’s most playful and popular concerto, followed by the fiery intensity of one of Tchaikovsky’s most beautiful and expressive orchestral works.

subtitle: Khatia Buniatishvili - Ibert, Honegger, Brahms
description: From the luminous elegance of Ibert’s Escales to the grandeur of Honegger’s Third Symphony and the fiery intensity of a landmark concerto—Jonathan Nott and Khatia Buniatishvili champion a programme of striking contrasts.

subtitle: Stefan Dohr - Debussy, Salonen, Sibelius
description: Having displayed all his conducting finesse in Debussy's luminous hedonism, Esa-Pekka Salonen transforms himself into a composer for an exceptional premiere, before celebrating Finland's eternal bard in Sibelius.

title: Sur l’eau
subtitle: Orchestre national d’Île-de-France - Ainārs Rubiķis - Alice Coote - Smetana, Elgar, Brahms
description: Water has always fascinated composers, as reflected in the river-inspired lyricism of Smetana’s most famous work and Elgar’s evocative seascapes. The evening culminates with the majestic power of Brahms’s Third Symphony.

title: Orchestre de Paris / Esa-Pekka Salonen
subtitle: Renaud Capuçon - R. Strauss, Bartók, Sibelius
description: Framed by the near-pictural precision of Strauss and the exhilarating breath of Sibelius, Bartók's diabolical Concerto No.2 crackles under the bow of Renaud Capuçon!
